7
Directions: Limitations
Prognostication
"Without a reliable and complete system of directing, all natal
astrology tends to remain vague and abstract". Having stated
this candidly, Mr. Carter proceeds to observe that "all candid
astrologers will admit that none of our present systems give
entirely satisfactory results". In his, 'Introduction to Radix
System', Mr. Robson pleads for a method which can combine
"the wealth of directions and the definiteness of predictions of
the primary system with the ease of the working of the secon
dary". Some systems of directing take a day after birth as
equivalent to one year. Others progress the Sun and the M.C.
by one degree or 59'8" per year after birth. Still others take the
solar return or the lunar return or both. When we look at the
